title: manulea crassifolia subsp. thodeana
intro: Manulea crassifolia subsp. thodeana, also known as the Manulea or Cape plumbago, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, highlighting its versatility in traditional medicine.
